item.title: Hello hugo
item.description: This site and blog has moved to use Hugo and Github pages. When I registered my domain khalid.se, I was aiming to even have it as a itty.bitty site and be truly serverless. However, I wanted to take on the challenge to host the whole infrastructure by my self to get a real feeling of the challenges of setting up a server, from the OS to the actual HTML files.

item.title: About me
item.description: I am a IT and security manager at an international NGO in Sweden, and this is where I publish my presentations of intrest, my contact information and do some crazy stuff behind the scenes. What I like doing Working out Socializing Hiking and being in nature Advocate for digital privacy Solving old and new problems in new ways What I’m doing now Some of my projects are listed here, this section is updated quaterly

item.title: Contact
item.description: You are welcome to reach out to me on the following links: 🧑🏽‍💻 Github 🧑🏽‍💼 LinkedIn 🐘 Mastodon 📧 khalid-se.br9ty[at]slmail.me 🔐 PGP public key Work of interest 👁️‍🗨️ Surveillance based ads, and their impact on society (Handouts, in Swedish) link. ✊🏽 Cybersecurity for activists, a seminar covering Cybersecurity basics. link 🏳️‍🌈 Oppressed Pride , master thesis in Social Work. link

item.title: Are endusers really the weakest link?
item.description: I’ve recently started a cybersecurity course in university and got very engaged by an assignment where I was to make a list on how to reduce the human factor when dealing with cyber security, and wrote these thoughts. I’d like to delve deeper into an idea within cybersecurity professionals that “a chain is only as strong as its weakest link” that often is pointingat the end-users as that weakest link (1).
